content: Hey Saturday, quick update: I had my first coaching session with Rachel Goldstein yesterday about my public speaking.
That’s a great start—Rachel Goldstein is excellent for practical speaking drills. What did she focus on in the first session, and do you have a specific talk coming up?
Yeah, she flagged my filler words (mostly “um” and “like”), plus I tend to stare at one spot instead of scanning the room. I’ve got a 15-minute presentation next quarter for about 50 people.

Super clear goals, and that audience size is perfect for practicing room coverage. Try dividing the room into left-center-right sections and intentionally holding eye contact in each for one full sentence. For filler words, pause silently instead of saying “um” or “like”—it sounds more confident.
Makes sense. She also wants me to slow down to around 130 words per minute, because I rush when I’m nervous. I’m practicing at Toastmasters every 2 weeks now.

Great plan—Toastmasters every 2 weeks gives you a solid feedback loop. Record a 2-minute segment and check your word count so you can calibrate to ~130 wpm, then mark intentional pause points in your script. That will help pace and reduce rush energy.
Love that. Last thing: Rachel said my content needs better storytelling structure so it doesn’t feel like random points. Can you give me a simple framework I can use for this 15-minute talk?

Absolutely—use a simple arc: Hook (1 min), Problem (3 min), Journey/Attempts (5 min), Resolution (4 min), Clear takeaway + CTA (2 min). Keep one core message and tie each section back to it with a short transition line. If you want, I can help you outline your exact talk next.
